description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular block of stone set atop a slightly larger stone base, forming a two-tiered structure. The upper block has a rough, uneven top surface and weathered edges, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. The stone is predominantly gray, with subtle variations in tone and patches of moss or lichen at the base, indicating outdoor placement. One side features faint, worn inscriptions or engravings, hinting at historical or functional significance. The surfaces are coarse and irregular, with visible chipping and erosion, especially along the corners and edges. The overall appearance is utilitarian and solid, designed to serve as a protective barrier or marker in an urban or historical setting.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone guard set on a larger stone base, with rough surfaces, chipped edges, and faint engravings on one side. The object is predominantly gray with signs of outdoor aging and moss at the base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broad, flat face of the upper stone block, with a rough top and a clean, unmarked surface.
description_view_1: The right side reveals faint, worn inscriptions or engravings on the stone, with visible chipping along the top edge.
description_view_2: The back view displays a mostly unmarked, rough stone surface with vertical striations and some discoloration.
description_view_3: The left side shows additional weathering and faint markings, with more pronounced moss growth at the base.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near intersections, driveways, or historical sites to protect corners or mark boundaries. Often found adjacent to sidewalks or at the entrance to pedestrian zones.
attribute_installation_orientation: Intended to be fully above ground, with the base resting flush on a paved or compacted surface, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion along the top edges and corners, likely caused by vehicle contact and weathering. Discoloration and moss growth at the base from prolonged exposure to moisture and foot traffic. Surface smoothing in areas of repeated contact.
attribute_historical_markings: Faint, worn inscriptions or engravings are visible on one side, possibly indicating historical or functional information. The markings are shallow and partially eroded, making them difficult to read.
